name: Magnesium Glycinate 400mg
blurb: Magnesium glycinate supports GABA-A receptor activity for deeper sleep onset, dampens HPA-axis cortisol surges, and maintains bone mineral density — three systems that decline in tandem with progesterone during perimenopause.
contraindications: Consult provider if on antibiotics or if kidney function is impaired.

name: Vitamin D3 + K2 (D3 5000 IU / K2 MK-7 200mcg)
blurb: D3 + K2 together direct calcium into bone rather than arterial walls — critical during estrogen decline when osteoclast activity outpaces formation, reducing fracture risk shown on DEXA.
contraindications: Consult provider if on warfarin (K2 interaction) or hypercalcaemia.

name: Omega-3 EPA/DHA 2g Daily
blurb: Omega-3s lower triglycerides, reduce systemic inflammation, and support neuronal membrane fluidity — all pathways implicated in mood volatility and cardiovascular risk that rise as estradiol withdraws.
contraindications: Consult provider if on anticoagulants or before surgery.

name: Adaptogen Blend (Ashwagandha KSM-66 + Rhodiola 3%)
blurb: KSM-66 ashwagandha blunts the HPA-axis cortisol surge that amplifies vasomotor events and displaces progesterone; rhodiola supports adrenal resilience during the stress-flash cycle documented in Category 1 and 2.
contraindications: Avoid if pregnant. Consult provider if on thyroid medication or immunosuppressants.

name: Phytoestrogen Complex (Red Clover Isoflavones 80mg + Soy Isoflavones)
blurb: Red clover isoflavones bind ERβ receptors to modestly buffer the thermoregulatory instability of estradiol decline; efficacy depends on gut estrobolome converting them to active equol.
contraindications: Not recommended with estrogen-sensitive cancers. Confirm with oncologist or NAMS practitioner before use.

name: Marine Collagen Peptides 10g
blurb: Estrogen directly stimulates dermal fibroblast collagen synthesis; marine collagen peptides provide the substrate to partially offset the 30% collagen loss seen in the first five years post-menopause.
contraindications: Fish allergy — use plant-based alternative.

name: Women's Probiotic + Prebiotic (Lactobacillus reuteri + rhamnosus, 50B CFU)
blurb: The gut estrobolome — beta-glucuronidase-producing bacteria — controls circulating estrogen recycling; Lactobacillus strains with beta-glucuronidase activity support estrogen reactivation and reduce mood-disrupting metabolite accumulation.
contraindications: Consult provider if immunocompromised or recovering from acute GI illness.

name: Calcium Citrate 500mg (with Boron 3mg)
blurb: Calcium citrate is absorbed independent of stomach acid — critical for peri/post-menopausal women on PPIs; boron slows bone resorption and supports estradiol recycling when ovarian output is declining.
contraindications: Do not exceed 1200mg calcium daily from all sources. Kidney stone history — discuss with provider.

name: Active B-Complex (Methylfolate + P5P + Methylcobalamin)
blurb: Active B vitamins drive the methylation cycle that recycles serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine; methylcobalamin and P5P support BDNF expression which declines with estradiol and underpins cognitive sharpness.
contraindications: High-dose B6 (>100mg) can cause peripheral neuropathy — avoid megadoses.

name: Resistance Band Set (5-Level, Loop + Long Bands)
blurb: Progressive resistance training is the single highest-evidence intervention for preserving bone mineral density post-menopause — equivalent to bisphosphonate-level protection in meta-analyses; bands enable safe progressive overload at home.

name: Cooling Pillow Protector / Mattress Pad (Phase-Change Material)
blurb: Phase-change material absorbs body heat during nocturnal vasomotor events, keeping skin surface temperature below the 1°C flash threshold — a non-pharmacological intervention that measurably reduces night waking frequency.

name: Mulberry Silk Pillowcase (22 Momme)
blurb: Silk generates 43% less friction than cotton against estrogen-thinned hair and reduced-collagen skin — preventing mechanical breakage at the follicle neck and sleep-crease wrinkles that worsen as dermal collagen falls.

name: Non-Hormonal Vaginal Moisturiser (Hyaluronic Acid + Vitamin E, pH-matched)
blurb: Estrogen loss halts glycogen secretion from vaginal epithelium, raising pH and thinning mucosa; daily hyaluronic acid moisturiser restores moisture barrier and reduces microtrauma without systemic hormone exposure.

name: Pelvic Floor Biofeedback Trainer (App-Connected)
blurb: Biofeedback-guided pelvic floor training reduces stress urinary incontinence episodes by up to 70% in randomised trials — first-line before pharmacology for GSM-related leakage as urethral collagen thins post-menopause.
contraindications: Contraindicated in acute pelvic infection or post-surgical healing. Confirm with urogynecologist.

name: Topical Retinol Serum 0.3% (Encapsulated, Buffered)
blurb: Retinol stimulates fibroblast collagen I and III synthesis and accelerates keratinocyte turnover — directly compensating for the estrogen-withdrawal collagen deficit at the dermal level.
contraindications: Avoid during pregnancy. Use SPF daily. Start 2x weekly to assess tolerance.

name: SPF 30+ Daily Moisturiser (Mineral, Ceramide-Enhanced)
blurb: Daily SPF prevents UV-driven collagen degradation that compounds the estrogen-loss collagen deficit; ceramides replenish the impaired skin barrier function seen as sebaceous gland activity declines with androgens.

name: Berberine HCl 500mg
blurb: Berberine activates AMPK — the same target as metformin — improving insulin sensitivity and reducing visceral adiposity that accelerates with the metabolic shift of estrogen withdrawal; also modulates gut microbiome beta-glucuronidase.
contraindications: Consult provider if on metformin, diabetes medications, or statins. Not for use in pregnancy.

name: Creatine Monohydrate 5g
blurb: Creatine supplementation in peri/menopausal women increases lean mass, bone density, and phosphocreatine-dependent cognitive processing speed — all domains that see accelerated decline with estrogen loss.
contraindications: Consult provider if chronic kidney disease. Ensure adequate hydration.

name: MCT Oil C8/C10 (Brain & Energy Support)
blurb: C8 MCT converts rapidly to ketones that bypass glucose metabolism, providing an alternative brain fuel when estradiol-dependent glucose uptake declines — directly targeting the brain fog and processing speed loss of perimenopause.
contraindications: Start with 1 tsp to assess GI tolerance. Not appropriate as sole fat source.

name: Evening Primrose Oil 1000mg (GLA 8%)
blurb: Gamma-linolenic acid in evening primrose oil is a prostaglandin E1 precursor that supports the skin moisture barrier and has modest evidence for reducing hot flash intensity when sebaceous output drops with androgens.
contraindications: Consult provider if on anticoagulants or anti-platelet drugs.

name: Low-Dose Melatonin 0.5mg (Prolonged Release)
blurb: Low-dose melatonin (0.5mg) reduces sleep onset latency without next-day grogginess — physiological replacement as menopause-related cortisol elevation suppresses endogenous pineal melatonin secretion in the evening window.
contraindications: May potentiate sedative medications. Consult provider if on antidepressants.

name: Zinc Picolinate 15mg + Copper 1mg Balance
blurb: Zinc is a co-factor in 5-alpha reductase regulation and keratinocyte proliferation — deficiency accelerates the androgenic alopecia pattern that emerges when testosterone-to-estradiol ratio shifts in perimenopause.
contraindications: Always pair with copper to prevent copper depletion. Do not exceed 40mg zinc daily.

name: Electrolyte Replenishment Blend (Low-Sugar, Sodium + Potassium + Magnesium)
blurb: Drenching night sweats and daytime flashes create a recurring electrolyte deficit that compounds HRV decline and amplifies palpitations; low-sugar electrolyte replacement stabilises autonomic tone throughout the day.
contraindications: Consult provider if on blood pressure medications or have renal impairment.

name: Vitex (Chaste Tree Berry) 400mg Standardised Extract
blurb: Vitex acts on pituitary dopamine receptors to support luteal-phase progesterone output — directly targeting the progesterone deficiency that drives sleep fragmentation, cyclical anxiety, and mood instability in early perimenopause.
contraindications: Not for use with hormone therapy or dopaminergic drugs. Do not use in post-menopause.

name: Black Cohosh Extract 40mg (Standardised Triterpene Glycosides)
blurb: Black cohosh triterpene glycosides interact with serotonin 5-HT7 receptors in the hypothalamic thermoregulatory centre, reducing hot flash frequency and intensity without estrogenic signalling — clinically studied for up to 12 months.
contraindications: Avoid with hepatotoxic medications. Consult provider if history of liver disease or estrogen-sensitive conditions.
